{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bdd29bf2514541f0bcfd3bf6","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# OpenBao Disaster Recovery Runbook\n2\t\n3\t## Architecture Overview\n4\t\n5\t```\n6\tardenone-cluster ──┐\n7\tardenone-manager ──┤── cross-cluster replication (every 30 min, over headscale)\n8\tardenone-hub ──────┘\n9\t\n10\tardenone-cluster ──→ Garage S3 (local) ──→ Synology NAS    (restic, every 6h)\n11\tardenone-manager ──→ Garage S3 (local) ──→ Synology NAS    (restic, every 6h)\n12\tardenone-hub ──────→ config dump to own KV ──→ replicated   (no direct S3 backup)\n13\t```\n14\t\n15\tEach cluster's secrets at `secret/<cluster-name>/*` are replicated to both other clusters.\n16\tardenone-cluster and ardenone-manager also have restic backups to Garage S3 (backed by Synology NAS).\n17\tardenone-hub dumps its config (policies, auth, mounts) to its own KV, which gets replicated.\n18\t\n19\t---\n20\t\n21\t## Scenario 1: Restore ardenone-cluster OpenBao\n22\t\n23\t### If the PVC is lost but the cluster is running\n24\t\n25\t1. **Reinstall OpenBao** — ArgoCD will redeploy the Helm chart automatically if the pod is gone.\n26\t\n27\t2. **Initialize and unseal:**\n28\t   ```bash\n29\t   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- bao operator init -key-shares=1 -key-threshold=1 -format=json\n30\t   ```\n31\t   Save the unseal key and root token.\n32\t\n33\t3. **Unseal:**\n34\t   ```bash\n35\t   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- bao operator unseal <UNSEAL_KEY>\n36\t   ```\n37\t\n38\t4. **Enable KV v2:**\n39\t   ```bash\n40\t   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- env VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 VAULT_TOKEN=<ROOT_TOKEN> bao secrets enable -path=secret kv-v2\n41\t   ```\n42\t\n43\t5. **Restore secrets from another cluster's OpenBao.**\n44\t   The other two clusters have copies of `ardenone-cluster/*` secrets. From a machine that can reach another cluster's OpenBao:\n45\t\n46\t   ```bash\n47\t   # List all ardenone-cluster secrets on ardenone-manager\n48\t   curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $MANAGER_TOKEN\" \\\n49\t     http://openbao-ardenone-manager:8200/v1/secret/metadata/ardenone-cluster?list=true | jq\n50\t\n51\t   # For each secret, read from manager and write to the restored cluster\n52\t   # Read:\n53\t   curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $MANAGER_TOKEN\" \\\n54\t     http://openbao-ardenone-manager:8200/v1/secret/data/ardenone-cluster/<path> | jq '.data.data'\n55\t\n56\t   # Write to restored cluster:\n57\t   curl -sf -X POST -H \"X-Vault-Token: $NEW_ROOT_TOKEN\" \\\n58\t     -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n59\t     -d '{\"data\": <secret-data>}' \\\n60\t     http://openbao-ardenone-cluster:8200/v1/secret/data/ardenone-cluster/<path>\n61\t   ```\n62\t\n63\t6. **Restore from restic** (alternative, if other clusters are also down):\n64\t   ```bash\n65\t   # List snapshots\n72\t   restic snapshots\n73\t\n74\t   # Restore latest\n75\t   restic restore latest --target /tmp/openbao-restore\n76\t\n77\t   # Secrets are at /tmp/openbao-restore/tmp/openbao-dump/secrets/\n78\t   # Policies from restic dump\n85\t   for f in /tmp/openbao-restore/tmp/openbao-dump/policies/*.json; do\n86\t     name=$(basename \"$f\" .json)\n87\t     policy=$(jq -r '.data.policy' \"$f\")\n88\t     echo \"$policy\" | bao policy write \"$name\" -\n89\t   done\n90\t   ```\n91\t\n92\t8. **Recreate replicator tokens** — follow the setup in `~/scratch/openbao-replicator-setup.md`.\n93\t\n94\t9. **Restore sealed-secrets keys** — critical, must be done BEFORE starting the sealed-secrets controller:\n95\t   ```bash\n96\t   # Recreate the secret in the sealed-secrets namespace\n100\t   ```\n101\t\n102\t---\n103\t\n104\t## Scenario 2: Restore ardenone-manager OpenBao\n105\t\n106\tSame process as Scenario 1, but:\n107\t- Read `ardenone-manager/*` secrets from ardenone-cluster or ardenone-hub\n108\t- Restic repo is at `s3:<garage-endpoint>/openbao-backups/ardenone-manager`\n109\t- After restoring secrets, also restore the ESO bootstrap token:\n110\t  ```bash\n111\t  kubectl --context=ardenone-manager create secret generic openbao-eso-token \\\n112\t    --namespace=external-secrets \\\n113\t    --from-literal=token=<eso-token>\n114\t  ```\n115\t  The ESO token value is backed up at `secret/ardenone-manager/external-secrets/eso-token` on the other clusters.\n116\t\n117\t---\n118\t\n119\t## Scenario 3: Restore ardenone-hub OpenBao\n120\t\n121\tardenone-hub has no restic backup — restoration is from cross-cluster replication only.\n122\t\n123\t1. **Initialize, unseal, enable KV v2** (same as Scenario 1).\n124\t\n125\t2. **Restore secrets** from ardenone-cluster or ardenone-manager:\n126\t   ```bash\n127\t   # OpenBao Disaster Recovery Runbook\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n```\nardenone-cluster ──┐\nardenone-manager ──┤── cross-cluster replication (every 30 min, over headscale)\nardenone-hub ──────┘\n\nardenone-cluster ──→ Garage S3 (local) ──→ Synology NAS    (restic, every 6h)\nardenone-manager ──→ Garage S3 (local) ──→ Synology NAS    (restic, every 6h)\nardenone-hub ──────→ config dump to own KV ──→ replicated   (no direct S3 backup)\n```\n\nEach cluster's secrets at `secret/<cluster-name>/*` are replicated to both other clusters.\nardenone-cluster and ardenone-manager also have restic backups to Garage S3 (backed by Synology NAS).\nardenone-hub dumps its config (policies, auth, mounts) to its own KV, which gets replicated.\n\n---\n\n## Scenario 1: Restore ardenone-cluster OpenBao\n\n### If the PVC is lost but the cluster is running\n\n1. **Reinstall OpenBao** — ArgoCD will redeploy the Helm chart automatically if the pod is gone.\n\n2. **Initialize and unseal:**\n   ```bash\n   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- bao operator init -key-shares=1 -key-threshold=1 -format=json\n   ```\n   Save the unseal key and root token.\n\n3. **Unseal:**\n   ```bash\n   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- bao operator unseal <UNSEAL_KEY>\n   ```\n\n4. **Enable KV v2:**\n   ```bash\n   kubectl --context=ardenone-cluster exec -n openbao openbao-ardenone-cluster-0 -- env VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 VAULT_TOKEN=<ROOT_TOKEN> bao secrets enable -path=secret kv-v2\n   ```\n\n5. **Restore secrets from another cluster's OpenBao.**\n   The other two clusters have copies of `ardenone-cluster/*` secrets. From a machine that can reach another cluster's OpenBao:\n\n   ```bash\n   # List all ardenone-cluster secrets on ardenone-manager\n   curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $MANAGER_TOKEN\" \\\n     http://openbao-ardenone-manager:8200/v1/secret/metadata/ardenone-cluster?list=true | jq\n\n   # For each secret, read from manager and write to the restored cluster\n   # Read:\n   curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $MANAGER_TOKEN\" \\\n     http://openbao-ardenone-manager:8200/v1/secret/data/ardenone-cluster/<path> | jq '.data.data'\n\n   # Write to restored cluster:\n   curl -sf -X POST -H \"X-Vault-Token: $NEW_ROOT_TOKEN\" \\\n     -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n     -d '{\"data\": <secret-data>}' \\\n     http://openbao-ardenone-cluster:8200/v1/secret/data/ardenone-cluster/<path>\n   ```\n\n6. **Restore from restic** (alternative, if other clusters are also down):\n   ```bash\n   # On a machine with access to the Garage S3 bucket\n   export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<key>\n   export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<secret>\n   export RESTIC_REPOSITORY=\"s3:<garage-endpoint>/openbao-backups/ardenone-cluster\"\n   export RESTIC_PASSWORD=<restic-password>\n\n   # List snapshots\n   restic snapshots\n\n   # Restore latest\n   restic restore latest --target /tmp/openbao-restore\n\n   # Secrets are at /tmp/openbao-restore/tmp/openbao-dump/secrets/\n   # Policies at /tmp/openbao-restore/tmp/openbao-dump/policies/\n   # Replay them via the OpenBao API\n   ```\n\n7. **Recreate policies and auth** — use the restic dump or recreate from the declarative-config templates:\n   ```bash\n   # Policies from restic dump\n   for f in /tmp/openbao-restore/tmp/openbao-dump/policies/*.json; do\n     name=$(basename \"$f\" .json)\n     policy=$(jq -r '.data.policy' \"$f\")\n     echo \"$policy\" | bao policy write \"$name\" -\n   done\n   ```\n\n8. **Recreate replicator tokens** — follow the setup in `~/scratch/openbao-replicator-setup.md`.\n\n9. **Restore sealed-secrets keys** — critical, must be done BEFORE starting the sealed-secrets controller:\n   ```bash\n   # Read from another cluster's OpenBao\n   curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $TOKEN\" \\\n     http://openbao-ardenone-manager:8200/v1/secret/data/sealed-secrets/ardenone-cluster/<key-name>\n   # Recreate the secret in the sealed-secrets namespace\n   ```\n\n---\n\n## Scenario 2: Restore ardenone-manager OpenBao\n\nSame process as Scenario 1, but:\n- Read `ardenone-manager/*` secrets from ardenone-cluster or ardenone-hub\n- Restic repo is at `s3:<garage-endpoint>/openbao-backups/ardenone-manager`\n- After restoring secrets, also restore the ESO bootstrap token:\n  ```bash\n  kubectl --context=ardenone-manager create secret generic openbao-eso-token \\\n    --namespace=external-secrets \\\n    --from-literal=token=<eso-token>\n  ```\n  The ESO token value is backed up at `secret/ardenone-manager/external-secrets/eso-token` on the other clusters.\n\n---\n\n## Scenario 3: Restore ardenone-hub OpenBao\n\nardenone-hub has no restic backup — restoration is from cross-cluster replication only.\n\n1. **Initialize, unseal, enable KV v2** (same as Scenario 1).\n\n2. **Restore secrets** from ardenone-cluster or ardenone-manager:\n   ```bash\n   # All hub secrets are at ardenone-cluster:/secret/data/ardenone-hub/*\n   # and ardenone-manager:/secret/data/ardenone-hub/*\n   ```\n\n3. **Restore config** — the config dump agent stores policies/auth/mounts at:\n   - `secret/ardenone-hub/openbao-config/policies`\n   - `secret/ardenone-hub/openbao-config/auth-methods`\n   - `secret/ardenone-hub/openbao-config/mounts`\n\n   Read these from another cluster and replay:\n   ```bash\n   # Get policies dump from ardenone-cluster\n   policies=$(curl -sf -H \"X-Vault-Token: $CLUSTER_TOKEN\" \\\n     http://openbao-ardenone-cluster:8200/v1/secret/data/ardenone-hub/openbao-config/policies | \\\n     jq '.data.data')\n\n   # Write each policy\n   echo \"$policies\" | jq -r 'to_entries[] | \"\\(.key)\\t\\(.value)\"' | while IFS=$'\\t' read name content; do\n     echo \"$content\" | curl -sf -X PUT -H \"X-Vault-Token: $HUB_ROOT_TOKEN\" \\\n       -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n       -d \"{\\\"policy\\\":\\\"$(echo \"$content\" | jq -sRr @json | sed 's/^\"//;s/\"$//')\\\"}\" \\\n       http://openbao-ardenone-hub:8200/v1/sys/policies/acl/$name\n   done\n   ```\n\n4. **Recreate replicator tokens** — follow `~/scratch/openbao-replicator-setup.md`.\n\n---\n\n## Scenario 4: Total loss (all three clusters)\n\n1. Restore from restic (Garage S3 on Synology NAS).\n2. Pick one cluster to restore first (ardenone-manager recommended — it has ESO and ArgoCD).\n3. Follow Scenario 2 for ardenone-manager.\n4. Once manager is up with replication running, restore the other clusters — the replicator will sync data to them.\n\n---\n\n## Key credentials to keep safe (outside of OpenBao)\n\nStore these in a password manager or offline backup:\n\n| Secret | Purpose | Stored where |\n|---|---|---|\n| Unseal keys (one per cluster) | Unseal OpenBao after restart | Password manager |\n| Root tokens (one per cluster) | Admin access for DR | Password manager |\n| Restic passwords (one per cluster) | Decrypt restic backups | Password manager + cross-cluster OpenBao |\n| Garage S3 credentials | Access restic backups on S3 | Garage operator auto-generates; backed up in OpenBao |\n| ESO bootstrap token (manager) | Bootstrap ESO on ardenone-manager | `secret/ardenone-manager/external-secrets/eso-token` |\n| Headscale auth keys | Re-register headscale clients | Headscale server on ardenone-hub |\n\n---\n\n## Verification checklist\n\nRun periodically to confirm backups are healthy:\n\n```bash\n# Check replicator logs on each cluster\nkubectl --context=ardenone-manager logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-replicator --tail=5\nkubectl --context=ardenone-cluster logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-replicator --tail=5\n# Hub via SSH:\nssh root@91.99.122.6 'k3s kubectl logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-replicator --tail=5'\n\n# Check restic backup logs\nkubectl --context=ardenone-manager logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-restic-backup --tail=5\nkubectl --context=ardenone-cluster logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-restic-backup --tail=5\n\n# Check hub config dump\nssh root@91.99.122.6 'k3s kubectl logs -n openbao deploy/openbao-config-dump --tail=5'\n\n# List restic snapshots\nrestic -r s3:<endpoint>/openbao-backups/ardenone-manager snapshots\nrestic -r s3:<endpoint>/openbao-backups/ardenone-cluster snapshots\n```\n","numLines":202,"startLine":1,"totalLines":202}}}
